content.problemStatement:
You love array, don't you?

You are given two integer NNN and MMM, along with an array BBB of length NNN containing **pairwise distinct** integers. You have to find the number of array AAA of length NNN satisfying the follow conditions:


1‚Č§Ai‚Č§M1 \leq A_i \leq M1‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§M.
Ai‚Č†AjA_i \neq A_jAi‚ÄčÓÄ†=Aj‚Äč for all 1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N1 \le i \lt j \le N1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N.
Ai‚Č†BiA_i \neq B_iAi‚ÄčÓÄ†=Bi‚Äč for all 1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N.
Since the answer can be very large, output it under modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The second line of the input contains NNN separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \dots, B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč - the array BBB.


Output Format
Output on a single line the number of satisfactory array modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: Bi‚Č†BjB_i \neq B_jBi‚ÄčÓÄ†=Bj‚Äč for all 1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N1 \le i \lt j \le N1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
The only satisfactory array is:

[2,1][2, 1][2,1]
